zh.lifeSummary: 當Isabel Francis於大約1388年出生在英國德比夏爾時，她的父親Robert Fraunceys Sheriff of Nottinghamshire30歲，母親Isabel Bernehaud25歲。她於1403年與Sir Gervase Clifton在英國英格蘭諾廷加姆爾克里夫頓結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和6個女兒的父母。她去世於1457年6月13日在英國諾廷加姆爾享年70歲，埋葬在英國諾廷加姆爾克里夫頓。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Isabel Francis于大约1388年出生在英国德比夏爾时，她的父亲Robert Fraunceys Sheriff of Nottinghamshire30岁，母亲Isabel Bernehaud25岁。她于1403年与Sir Gervase Clifton在英国英格兰諾廷加姆爾克里夫頓结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和6个女儿的父母。她去世于1457年6月13日在英国諾廷加姆爾享年70岁，埋葬在英国諾廷加姆爾克里夫頓。

nameMeanings.name: Francis
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Old French personal name Franceis, modern French François, from Latin Franciscus, originally ‘Frank’, though later used to denote a Frenchman. The personal name owed much of its popularity during the Middle Ages to the fame of Saint Francis of Assisi (1181–1226), whose baptismal name was actually Giovanni but who was nicknamed Francisco because his father was absent in France at the time of his birth. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, e.g. Italian Francesco and Polish, Czech, Slovak, Slovenian, and Croatian Franc , and also their derivatives, e.g. Croatian Francišković (see Francisco 4).
English: ethnic name from the Old French adjective Franceis ‘French’ (see 1 above).
Americanized form of one or more similar (like-sounding) Jewish surnames, or an adoption of the non-Jewish surname.

variants.name: Ferenc
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Andrzej, Casimir, Iwan, Zbigniew.
Hungarian, Polish, Czech, Slovak, Rusyn (from Slovakia and Poland), Slovenian, and Croatian: from the personal name Ferenc, a Hungarian form of Latin Franciscus (see Francis ).

variants.name: Fraczek
meanings: Polish (Frączek): from a pet form of the personal name Franciszek, an equivalent of Latin Franciscus (see Francis ). It is found mainly in Lesser Poland.

variants.name: Chico
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Jesus, Pedro, Rafael, Bernardo, Camilo, Cirilo, Juan, Miguel, Ramona, Ruben.
Spanish: from chico ‘small, young’ (of uncertain origin, perhaps from Latin ciccum ‘trifle’), applied as a nickname for a small man, or for the younger of two bearers of the same personal name.
Portuguese: from a pet form of the personal name Francisco, a vernacular form of Franciscus (see Francis ).

variants.name: Francesco
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Gabriele, Giovanni, Pasquale, Rocco, Salvatore.  Spanish Carlota, Garcia, Romana.
Italian: from the personal name Francesco (from Latin Franciscus), an equivalent of Francis .

variants.name: Di Cicco
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Antonio, Pasquale, Carlo, Cosmo, Giovanni, Giulio, Giuseppe, Nicola, Attilio, Benedetto, Carmela.
Italian: patronymic from a pet form of the personal name Francesco, from Latin Franciscus (see Francis ).

variants.name: Ceccarelli
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Mario, Dino, Angelo, Dante, Quinto, Sesto, Aldo, Americo, Amerigo, Arcangelo, Augusto, Aurelio, Franco, Gino, Guido, Italo, Leoncio, Renato, Roberto, Tino, Tito, Valerio.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of the personal name Ceccarello, a diminutive of Cecco, itself a diminutive Francesco, from Latin Franciscus (see Francis ).

variants.name: Di Cioccio
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Cosmo, Mario, Cesidio, Dante, Guido, Salvatore, Aldo, Americo, Benito, Biagio, Domenico, Ezio, Flavio, Nunzio, Quintino, Silverio.
Italian: patronymic from a personal name of uncertain derivation,possibly a pet form of Francesco (see Francis ).

variants.name: Cillo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Americo, Aniello, Antonio, Carmine, Gianna, Giovanni, Guido, Livio, Rocco, Vito.
Italian: from a shortened pet form of Francesco (equivalent of Francis ).

variants.name: Cisco
meanings: Italian (Veneto): from the personal name Cisco, a short form of Francesco, Latin Franciscus (see Francis ).
American shortened form of the surname   Francisco   (which is in part of German and presumably also of French origin) and thus a variant of   Sisco   and   Sesco  .

variants.name: Fran
meanings: Croatian: from a short form of local equivalents of the Latin personal name Franciscus (see Francis ).
Spanish: probably an altered form of Catalan   Franc  .
Hispanic (mainly Philippines): from a short form of the Spanish personal name   Franco   or   Francisco  .

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabell
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Isobella
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isobelle
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isobel
meanings: Variant spelling of   Isabel .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Isabel Francis was born about 1388, in Derbyshire, England, her father, Robert Fraunceys Sheriff of Nottinghamshire, was 30 and her mother, Isabel Bernehaud, was 25. She married Sir Gervase Clifton in 1403, in Clifton, Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 6 daughters. She died on 13 June 1457, in Nottinghamshire, England, at the age of 70, and was buried in Clifton, Nottinghamshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Isabel Francis est née environ 1388, à Derbyshire, Angleterre, son père, Robert Fraunceys Sheriff of Nottinghamshire, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Isabel Bernehaud, avait 25 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Gervase Clifton en 1403, à Clifton, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 6 filles. Elle est décédée le 13 juin 1457, à Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 70 ans, et a été enterrée à Clifton, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre.

Sie hat 1403 in Clifton, Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom Sir Gervase Clifton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 6 Töchter. Sie starb am 13. Juni 1457 im Alter von 70 Jahren in Nottinghamshire, England und wurde in Clifton, Nottinghamshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Isabel Francis è nata il circa 1388 nel Derbyshire, England, sua padre, Robert Fraunceys Sheriff of Nottinghamshire, aveva 30 anni e sua madre, Isabel Bernehaud, aveva 25 anni. Lei sposato Sir Gervase Clifton nel 1403, nel Clifton, Nottinghamshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 6 figlie. Morì il 13 giugno 1457, nel Nottinghamshire, England, all'età di 70 anni, e fu sepolta a Clifton, Nottinghamshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Isabel Francisが約1388年にイギリスデルブイシュアイアで生まれた時，父Robert Fraunceys Sheriff of Nottinghamshireは30歳，母Isabel Bernehaudは25歳であった。1403年にイギリスイギリスノッティンガムシュアイアクリフトンでSir Gervase Cliftonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と6人の娘の親であった。彼女は1457年6月13日にイギリスノッティンガムシュアイアで70歳で死去し，イギリスノッティンガムシュアイアクリフトンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Isabel Francis(이)가 대략 1388년 영국 더비실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Robert Fraunceys Sheriff of Nottinghamshire(은)는 30세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Isabel Bernehaud(은)는 25세였다. 그녀는 1403년 영국 잉글랜드 노틴그함실르 클리프턴에서 Sir Gervase Clifton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1457년 6월 13일 영국 노틴그함실르에서 70세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 노틴그함실르 클리프턴에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Isabel Francis nasceu aproximadamente 1388, em Derbyshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Robert Fraunceys Sheriff of Nottinghamshire, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Isabel Bernehaud, tinha 25 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Gervase Clifton em 1403, em Clifton, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 6 filhas. Ela faleceu em 13 de junho de 1457, em Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra, com 70 anos, e foi sepultada em Clifton, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Isabel Francis родилась около 1388 г. в местности дирбишир, Англия, её отцу по имени Robert Fraunceys Sheriff of Nottinghamshire было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Isabel Bernehaud, 25. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Gervase Clifton в 1403 г. в местности клифтон, ноттингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 6 дочерей. Она умерла 13 июня 1457 г. в местности ноттингхамшир, Англия в возрасте 70 лет и была похоронена в местности клифтон, ноттингхамшир, Англия.

Se casó con Sir Gervase Clifton en 1403, en Clifton, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 6 hijas. Murió el 13 de junio de 1457, en Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 70 años, y fue sepultada en Clifton, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra.
